guide.input_std_concept_desc: In WIM UI, we have unified the structure of the "right icon area" for all input and selection components. This ensures a consistent user experience without layout breakage, even when clear buttons, dropdown icons, and validation icons coexist.
guide.input_std_desc: Guidelines for standardized icon placement rules and the `allowClear` functionality in input fields and various picker components.
guide.input_std_note_desc: ColorPicker does not support `allowClear` as an exception, because it triggers the browser's native color picker due to its `type="color"` nature.
guide.input_std_rule_1: To prevent icon overlap, the right icon area is managed via Flexbox, allowing multiple icons (clear, dropdown, others) to be displayed in parallel.
guide.input_std_rule_1_desc: To prevent icon overlap, the right icon area is managed with Flexbox, displaying multiple icons (Clear, Dropdown, Others) in parallel.
guide.input_std_rule_2: When allowClear is enabled, the 'x' button is displayed only when a value exists, allowing the value to be reset on click.
guide.input_std_rule_2_desc: When `allowClear` is enabled, the "X" button is displayed only when a value exists, allowing the value to be reset on click.
guide.input_std_rule_3: For elements with dropdowns like Selectboxes, the chevron icon is rotated 180 degrees based on the open/closed state to enhance visual feedback.
guide.input_std_rule_3_desc: For elements with dropdowns like Selectboxes, the chevron icon is rotated 180 degrees according to the open/closed state to enhance visual feedback.
guide.input_std_rule_4: Each picker (Time, Color, Date, etc.) is built based on Tier 2 Input components whenever possible, inheriting basic features.
guide.input_std_rule_4_desc: Each picker (Time, Color, Date, etc.) is built based on the Tier 2 Input component as much as possible to inherit basic features.
guide.rvc_desc: Guidelines for proper usage and differences between Radio, Checkbox, and Switch.
guide.rvc_design_checkbox: Square (■) with a checkmark (✔) when selected.
guide.rvc_design_radio: Circular (●) with a filled center when selected.
guide.rvc_design_switch: Capsule shape with a knob that slides to indicate On/Off.
guide.rvc_note_default: Radio buttons should have a default (e.g., standard shipping). However, leave unselected for surveys.
guide.rvc_note_vertical: Stack options vertically and make labels clickable (improved accessibility).
guide.rvc_selection_switch: Toggle between two states (On/Off). Independent setting.
guide.rvc_timing_checkbox: Requires Confirmation: Since multiple items are selected, an 'Apply' button flow is common.
guide.rvc_timing_radio: Immediate: Selecting one often triggers immediate setting changes or navigation.
guide.rvc_usage_checkbox: Checkbox Use Cases: Filters (Color/Size), Notifications (News/Updates), Terms agreement
guide.rvc_usage_radio: Radio Use Cases: Payment method (Credit/Bank), Sort order (Price/Newest), Delivery time
guide.rvc_usage_switch: Switch Use Cases: Enabling features, Toggling modes (e.g. Dark Mode). Immediate effects.
guide.rvc_vs_switch_checkbox: Switch for immediate effect. Checkbox if a save button is needed.
guide.rvc_vs_switch_radio: Radio for 3+ choices. Switch for 2 states (On/Off).
guide.textarea_std_concept_desc: Provides a UI that balances ease of input and information visibility in flexible long-form text entry, reducing input errors.
guide.textarea_std_desc: Defines common rules for components that handle multi-line input, such as Textarea and Mentions.
guide.textarea_std_note_desc: Use standard 'Input' for short entries and 'Textarea' for long text or comments. Consider using 'Mentions' when a response to specific keywords is required.
guide.textarea_std_rule_1: Size Adjustability: Supports not only fixed sizes but also `fieldSizing: content`, where the height expands automatically based on the input.
guide.textarea_std_rule_1_desc: Supports not only fixed sizes but also `fieldSizing: content`, where the height expands automatically based on the input.
guide.textarea_std_rule_2: Explicit States: Clearly indicate states such as input error (Error) or disabled (Disabled) through changes in borders or background colors.
guide.textarea_std_rule_2_desc: Clearly indicate states such as input error (Error) or disabled (Disabled) through changes in borders or background colors.
guide.textarea_std_rule_3: Integrated Functions: When adding special completion functions like Mentions (@) or Hashtags (#), ensure styles are common with the basic text area.
guide.textarea_std_rule_3_desc: When adding special completion functions like Mentions (@) or Hashtags (#), ensure styles are common with the basic text area.
guide.textarea_std_rule_4: Layout Optimization: Standardize width control through the `fullWidth` property to maintain consistency within forms.
guide.textarea_std_rule_4_desc: Standardize width control through the `fullWidth` property to maintain consistency within forms.

basicinput.guide_desc: Choose components for entering basic text or numerical values from users.
basicinput.input_usage: Name, email address, phone number, etc.
basicinput.mask_usage: Credit cards, dates, fixed format inputs.

formlabels.a11y_risk_desc: Without correct implementation, screen readers might not read labels properly.
formlabels.allowed_cases: Cases where usage is exceptionally allowed
formlabels.deprecated_policy: Not recommended in principle due to many UX challenges.
formlabels.floating: A design pattern where the label is shown inside the field like a placeholder initially, shrinking and moving to the top when focused
formlabels.guide: Guidelines regarding label placement in forms and the use of Floating Labels
formlabels.line_break: Can line-break depending on label length
formlabels.missing_info_desc: Since it occupies space inside the field when empty, there's no room for format examples (hints), making it hard to convey 'what' and 'how' simultaneously.
formlabels.mobile_space_desc: When the need to save height on mobile outweighs usability concerns.
formlabels.mobile_width: Can effectively utilize width on mobile screens
formlabels.placeholder_benefit: Can use placeholders as supplementary text.
formlabels.scannability_benefit: Smoothly check inputs just by moving eyes from top to bottom.
formlabels.top_align: WIM UI recommends Top-aligned, displaying the label above the input field
formlabels.visibility_benefit: Fixed label position and sufficient font size ensure readability.

formlayout.guide_desc: Elements to organize input fields and enhance accessibility and usability.
formlayout.inputgroup_desc: Adds icons or units before/after (Prefix/Suffix) the input field.
formlayout.label_desc: Explicitly identifies input item names. Click on labels to focus the field.
formlayout.label_necessity_desc: All input items should have a `Label`; do not use placeholders as a replacement.
formlayout.legend_desc: The title for items grouped by Fieldset.
formlayout.proximity_principle_desc: Place `FieldError` immediately near the input item where the error occurred.
formlayout.visual_grouping_desc: Use `Fieldset` for semantic groups like address or profile information.

formui.abandon_form: Frustration builds up if they don't know what to fix, causing form abandonment.
formui.auto_conv_details: Absorb notation variants (e.g., full-width to half-width).
formui.auto_conv_effect: Reduces user burden and prevents data inconsistency.
formui.autocomplete_effect: Skips manual input and significantly reduces typos.
formui.btn_activation_details: Disable button until required fields are filled, or validate on click.
formui.btn_activation_effect: Prevents accidental submission in incomplete states.
formui.burden: Users feel burdened and abandon the flow when excessive input items or irrelevant information are required.
formui.char_limit_details: State limits (e.g., "Max 100") and show real-time counts.
formui.char_limit_effect: Prevents post-input corrections and gives reassurance.
formui.clear_cta_details: Use specific labels like "Submit" or "Next" with visual emphasis.
formui.clear_cta_effect: Users can execute the next action without hesitation.
formui.count_opt_effect: Reduces click count and lessens user "tedium".
formui.count_opt_examples: Use radios for few choices; limit items to essentials.
formui.error_confusion: Users get confused if instructions are ambiguous or error messages aren't specific.
formui.external_link_details: Don't lead users away during input; use new tabs if needed.
formui.field_width_effect: Improves visual balance and provides hints (affordance).
formui.field_width_examples: Set widths matching content (e.g., zip code, address).
formui.form_guide: Guidelines for designing forms that are easy for users to input and complete without stress.
formui.help_text_details: Crucial info near field; supplementary info in tooltips.
formui.help_text_effect: Clarifies rules and reduces hesitation or errors.
formui.increase_conversion: Aims to increase conversion rates by preventing input mistakes and errors, and reducing psychological burden.
formui.info_grouping_effect: Makes info chunks easier to recognize and smoothes the flow.
formui.info_grouping_examples: Place personal info, address, etc., together.
formui.inline_validation_effect: Notice errors immediately and encourage on-the-spot fix.
formui.input_method_details: Use DatePickers or Dropdowns to reduce free text input.
formui.input_method_effect: Prevents format errors and maintains data consistency.
formui.label_top_effect: Reduces eye movement and increases visibility, improving input speed.
formui.loading_indicator_effect: Conveys that process is ongoing, providing reassurance.
formui.long_complex_form: Forms are too long or complex
formui.many_choices: Displaying too many choices at once obscures which ones are required, leading to misinputs.
formui.mobile_keypad: On mobile, set `input type` appropriately, such as showing the numeric keypad for phone number fields.
formui.optimal_keypad_effect: Reduces input stress for mobile users.
formui.placeholder_label_effect: Ensures users always know what to enter and maintains a11y.
formui.placeholder_not_label: Use placeholders as hints; never substitute for labels.
formui.req_opt_effect: Allows users to judge at a glance what must be entered.
formui.req_opt_marks: Clearly mark "Required" items and add "(Optional)" to others.
formui.spacing_effect: Clarifies relations between Label, Input, and Help Message.
formui.step_viz_effect: Shows the path to the goal, providing reassurance and completion.
formui.step_viz_stepper: Show progress bars (Stepper) for multi-page forms.
formui.tap_mistake: Buttons and inputs that are too small induce mistaps and operational errors.
formui.unresponsive: Users will give up on the input completely if the responsive design is inadequate.

picker.behavior_memorized_or_copy: User knows value or wants to paste
picker.color_input_usage: When users need to paste HEX/RGB codes directly.
picker.color_picker_usage: Intuitive selection from palettes or gradients for themes/designs.
picker.date_picker_usage: Specifying birthdays, reservations, or deadlines with a visual calendar.
picker.date_range_input_usage: Specifying two dates via text input without opening a calendar.
picker.date_range_usage: Hotel stay durations, aggregation periods, etc.
picker.disabled_dates_desc: Dates that cannot be selected (e.g., past dates) should be grayed out.
picker.invalid_state_desc: Clearly indicate invalid states (e.g., graying out past dates).
picker.kb_a11y_desc: Ensure users can navigate pickers via `Tab` and confirm via `Enter`.
picker.mobile_overlay_desc: On mobile, use `Dialog` or `BottomSheet` for pickers that would take too much space.
picker.preview_feedback_desc: Provide constant visual feedback for the selected value (like `ColorInput`).
picker.range_slider_usage: Specifying ranges like price, age, or time intervals.
picker.slider_usage: Best for intuitive adjustments like volume or brightness.
picker.time_picker_usage: Selecting HH:mm separately for appointments or business hours.

placeholder.as_label_dont_desc: Reducing labels to look cleaner (Item names disappear during input)
placeholder.avoid_transient_info: Avoid including information that disappears during input (when starting to type).
placeholder.concise_words: Use short words readable at a glance, and choose placement appropriately according to info importance and length.
placeholder.guide_desc: Placeholders are for displaying short hints or examples (samples) of what to enter in an input field.
placeholder.philosophy_desc: A placeholder is just a "hint". It displays a specific input format example instead of the item name (label).

selection.btn_group_behavior_desc: Grouping multiple related operations. Clicking triggers an action.
selection.btn_group_scene_desc: When grouping independent action buttons like 'Save / Duplicate / Delete' or 'Edit / Delete'.
selection.checkbox_description: Allows multiple selections (0 or more). Each item is independent.
selection.checkbox_group_want_confirm: Confirm multiple values at once via 'Save' or 'Submit'
selection.chip_visual_usage: Display or select tags in a visually emphasized format
selection.choose_one_confirm_later: Select one from few candidates and submit later
selection.default_value_description: Radios usually have a recommended value pre-selected. Checkboxes are usually unselected by default.
selection.deferred_description: Radios and Checkboxes are normally used in forms where states are confirmed by 'Save' or 'Submit'.
selection.guide_desc: Choose the optimal format based on the number of options and the nature of the input.
selection.immediacy_deferred_alt: If you don't want to confirm until 'Submit', use Radio Button
selection.immediacy_needed: Result should be reflected the moment it's selected
selection.immediate_description: Switches and SegmentedControls should be used when settings are saved or views changed instantly.
selection.in_content_filter: Filtering or switching display format within the same content
selection.major_nav: Primary navigation or large-scale switches in the app
selection.multi_item_usage: Select multiple items from a list
selection.nature_multi_alt: If selecting multiple, use Checkbox or Chips
selection.radio_checkbox_guide_link: Guide: Refer to Guides / Radio vs Checkbox for detailed comparison.
selection.radio_description: Always exactly one selection. One item must always be selected.
selection.radio_group_usage: Selecting one option from a few (2–5) items. (e.g., shipping date, sort order).
selection.radio_individual_usage: Individual options (when placed independently without grouping)
selection.radio_usecase: Payment method (Credit/Bank), Sort order (Price Low/High), shipping date.
selection.range_global_alt: If moving across major screens, use Bottom Navigation
selection.range_partial: Inside a screen or changing display format
selection.search_filter_usage: Search and narrow down from a vast amount of data
selection.seg_count_alt: If 5 or more, use Dropdown or Horizontal Scroll Tabs
selection.seg_vs_other: Since SegmentedControl shares a similar role with other components, appropriate usage is important.
selection.segmented_behavior_desc: Switching values or display modes. Always exactly one is selected.
selection.segmented_scene_desc: When deciding or changing one state, such as 'Week / Month View' or 'Asc / Desc Sort'.
selection.segmented_usecase: Switch view format (e.g., Week/Month), change sort order (e.g., Asc/Desc).
selection.smooth: makes eye movement much smoother and reduces mistaps on mobile.
selection.space_saving_usage: Select from a medium-sized list while saving space
selection.switch_description: Toggles between two states (ON/OFF). Ideal for binary states like enabling/disabling.
selection.switch_group_usage: Immediate changes for multiple notification settings, etc.
selection.switch_in_place: Switch display mode or filters on the spot
selection.switch_usage: Immediate changes for Wi-Fi, Dark Mode, Notification settings.
selection.switch_usecase: Wi-Fi or Bluetooth ON/OFF, Dark Mode switch, instant notification enable.
selection.tab_exclusive_desc: Each tab has dedicated content and usually does not overlap
selection.tap_area_description: Make label texts clickable and ensure sufficient hit area.
selection.toggle_group_usage: Filter conditions, mode switch, size selection.
selection.toggle_group_visual_usage: Select from visually emphasized button formats
